00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "class.h"
00027 #include "jstring.h"
00028 #include "memory.h"
00029 #include "thread.h"
00030 #include "utf8_string.h"
00031 #include "util.h"
00032 
00033 /******************************************************************************
00034  * Type definitions                                                           *
00035  ******************************************************************************/
00036 
00039 struct jstring_manager_t {
00040     struct class_t *str_cl; 
00041     struct class_t *char_array_cl; 
00042 
00043     uint32_t load; 
00044     uint32_t entries; 
00045     uint32_t capacity; 
00046     uint32_t init_capacity; 
00047     java_lang_String_t **buckets; 
00048 
00049     uint32_t lit_load; 
00050     uint32_t lit_entries; 
00051     uint32_t lit_capacity; 
00052     uint32_t lit_init_capacity; 
00053     java_lang_String_t **lit_buckets; 
00054 };
00055 
00057 typedef struct jstring_manager_t jstring_manager_t;
00058 
00059 /******************************************************************************
00060  * Local declarations                                                         *
00061  ******************************************************************************/
00062 
00064 static jstring_manager_t jsm;
00065 
00066 /******************************************************************************
00067  * Local function prototypes                                                  *
00068  ******************************************************************************/
00069 
00070 static void jsm_rehash(uint32_t);
00071 static void jsm_rehash_literals(uint32_t);
00072 
00073 static uint32_t jstring_hash(const uint16_t *, uint32_t, uint32_t);
00074 static bool jstring_equals(const java_lang_String_t *,
00075                            const java_lang_String_t *);
00076 
00077 /******************************************************************************
00078  * Java string manager implementation                                         *
00079  ******************************************************************************/
00080 
00086 void jsm_init(uint32_t log2cap, uint32_t load)
00087 {
00088     assert(log2cap != 0);
00089     assert(log2cap < 32);
00090     assert(load != 0);
00091 
00092     jsm.load = load;
00093     jsm.entries = 0;
00094     jsm.capacity = 1 << log2cap;
00095     jsm.init_capacity = 1 << log2cap;
00096     jsm.buckets = gc_malloc(jsm.capacity * sizeof(java_lang_String_t *));
00097 
00098     jsm.lit_load = load;
00099     jsm.lit_entries = 0;
00100     jsm.lit_capacity = 1 << log2cap;
00101     jsm.lit_init_capacity = 1 << log2cap;
00102     jsm.lit_buckets = gc_malloc(jsm.lit_capacity *
00103                                 sizeof(java_lang_String_t *));
00104 } // jsm_init()
00105 
00112 void jsm_set_classes(class_t *str_cl, class_t *char_array_cl)
00113 {
00114     jsm.str_cl = str_cl;
00115     jsm.char_array_cl = char_array_cl;
00116 } // jsm_init_cl()
00117 
00122 void jsm_mark( void )
00123 {
00124     java_lang_String_t *str;
00125     uint32_t i;
00126 
00127     for (i = 0; i < jsm.lit_capacity; i++) {
00128         str = jsm.lit_buckets[i];
00129 
00130         while (str != NULL) {
00131             gc_mark_reference(JAVA_LANG_STRING_PTR2REF(str));
00132             str = str->next;
00133         }
00134     }
00135 } // jsm_mark()
00136 
00141 void jsm_purge( void )
00142 {
00143     java_lang_String_t *prev;
00144     java_lang_String_t *curr;
00145     java_lang_String_t list;
00146     jword_t used = 0;
00147 
00148     for (size_t i = 0; i < jsm.capacity; i++) {
00149         prev = &list;
00150         list.next = NULL;
00151         curr = (java_lang_String_t *) jsm.buckets[i];
00152 
00153         while (curr != NULL) {
00154             if (header_is_marked(&(curr->header))) {
00155                 prev = curr;
00156                 used++;
00157             } else {
00158                 prev->next = curr->next;
00159             }
00160 
00161             curr = curr->next;
00162         }
00163 
00164         jsm.buckets[i] = list.next;
00165     }
00166 
00167     jsm.entries = used;
00168 } // jsm_purge()
00169 
00174 static void jsm_rehash(uint32_t capacity)
00175 {
00176     java_lang_String_t **buckets;
00177     java_lang_String_t *str;
00178     uint32_t hash;
00179 
00180     buckets = gc_malloc(capacity * sizeof(java_lang_String_t *));
00181 
00182     for (size_t i = 0; i < jsm.capacity; i++) {
00183         str = jsm.buckets[i];
00184 
00185         while (str != NULL) {
00186             java_lang_String_t *tmp;
00187 
00188             // Interned stings always have their cachedHashCode field set
00189             hash = str->cachedHashCode;
00190             hash &= capacity - 1;
00191 
00192             tmp = str->next;
00193             str->next = buckets[hash];
00194             buckets[hash] = str;
00195 
00196             str = tmp;
00197         }
00198     }
00199 
00200     gc_free(jsm.buckets);
00201     jsm.buckets = buckets;
00202     jsm.capacity = capacity;
00203 } // jsm_rehash()
00204 
00210 static void jsm_rehash_literals(uint32_t capacity)
00211 {
00212     java_lang_String_t **buckets;
00213     java_lang_String_t *str;
00214     uint32_t hash;
00215     uint32_t i;
00216 
00217     buckets = gc_malloc(capacity * sizeof(java_lang_String_t *));
00218 
00219     for (i = 0; i < jsm.lit_capacity; i++) {
00220         str = jsm.lit_buckets[i];
00221 
00222         while (str != NULL) {
00223             java_lang_String_t *tmp;
00224 
00225             // Interned stings always have their cachedHashCode field set
00226             hash = str->cachedHashCode;
00227             hash &= capacity - 1;
00228 
00229             tmp = str->next;
00230             str->next = buckets[hash];
00231             buckets[hash] = str;
00232 
00233             str = tmp;
00234         }
00235     }
00236 
00237     gc_free(jsm.lit_buckets);
00238     jsm.lit_buckets = buckets;
00239     jsm.lit_capacity = capacity;
00240 } // jsm_rehash_literals()
00241 
00247 java_lang_String_t *jstring_intern(java_lang_String_t *jstr)
00248 {
00249     java_lang_String_t *curr;
00250     uint16_t *data = array_get_data(jstr->value);
00251     uint32_t count = jstr->count;
00252     uint32_t offset = jstr->offset;
00253     uint32_t hash;
00254 
00255     if (jstr->cachedHashCode == 0) {
00256         jstr->cachedHashCode = jstring_hash(data, offset, count);
00257     }
00258 
00259     tm_lock();
00260 
00261     // Check in the literal table
00262     hash = jstr->cachedHashCode;
00263     hash &= jsm.lit_capacity - 1;
00264     curr = jsm.lit_buckets[hash];
00265 
00266     while (curr != NULL) {
00267         if (jstring_equals(jstr, curr)) {
00268             tm_unlock();
00269             return curr;
00270         } else {
00271             curr = curr->next;
00272         }
00273     }
00274 
00275     // Check in the intern table
00276     hash = jstr->cachedHashCode;
00277     hash &= jsm.capacity - 1;
00278     curr = jsm.buckets[hash];
00279 
00280     while (curr != NULL) {
00281         if (jstring_equals(jstr, curr)) {
00282             tm_unlock();
00283             return curr;
00284         } else {
00285             curr = curr->next;
00286         }
00287     }
00288 
00289     // We haven't found a matching string, add the current one to the manager
00290     hash = jstr->cachedHashCode;
00291     hash &= jsm.capacity - 1;
00292 
00293     jstr->next = jsm.buckets[hash];
00294     jsm.buckets[hash] = jstr;
00295     jsm.entries++;
00296 
00297     if (jsm.entries > jsm.capacity * jsm.load) {
00298         jsm_rehash(jsm.capacity * 2);
00299     } else if ((jsm.entries < (jsm.capacity / 2) * jsm.load)
00300                && (jsm.capacity > jsm.init_capacity))
00301     {
00302         /* Though we are adding an entry to the manager a collection might
00303          * have reclaimed some entries before so we also check if we must
00304          * shrink the manager, we don't do this in jsm_purge() because that
00305          * function is called during a garbage collection */
00306         jsm_rehash(jsm.capacity / 2);
00307     }
00308 
00309     tm_unlock();
00310     return jstr;
00311 } // jstring_intern()
00312 
00318 java_lang_String_t *jstring_create_literal(const char *src)
00319 {
00320     java_lang_String_t *str;
00321     uintptr_t value, ref;
00322     uint32_t len, hash, cached_hash_code;
00323     uint16_t *data, *str_data;
00324 
00325     if (!utf8_check(src)) {
00326         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00327                 "UTF8 string contains invalid characters");
00328     }
00329 
00330     len = utf8_to_java_length(src);
00331     value = gc_new_array_nonref(T_CHAR, len);
00332     data = array_get_data((array_t *) value);
00333     utf8_to_java(data, src);
00334     cached_hash_code = jstring_hash(data, 0, len);
00335 
00336     tm_lock();
00337     hash = cached_hash_code & (jsm.lit_capacity - 1);
00338 
00339     // Check if we can find this literal in the hash-table of interned strings
00340     str = (java_lang_String_t *) jsm.lit_buckets[hash];
00341 
00342     while (str != NULL) {
00343         // Note that the interned string must be a literal, thus permanenent
00344         if (str->count == len) {
00345             str_data = array_get_data(str->value);
00346 
00347             if (memcmp(data, str_data, len * sizeof(uint16_t)) == 0) {
00348                 // This literal is already present, just return it
00349                 tm_unlock();
00350                 return str;
00351             }
00352         }
00353 
00354         str = (java_lang_String_t *) str->next;
00355     }
00356 
00357     // Prevent the value array from being accidentally collected
00358     thread_push_root(&value);
00359     ref = gc_new(jsm.str_cl);
00360     str = JAVA_LANG_STRING_REF2PTR(ref);
00361     thread_pop_root();
00362 
00363     str->value = (array_t *) value;
00364     str->count = len;
00365     str->offset = 0;
00366     str->cachedHashCode = cached_hash_code;
00367 
00368     // Add the string to the literal hash-table
00369     str->next = jsm.lit_buckets[hash];
00370     jsm.lit_buckets[hash] = str;
00371     jsm.lit_entries++;
00372 
00373     if (jsm.lit_entries > (jsm.lit_capacity * jsm.lit_init_capacity)) {
00374         thread_push_root(&ref);
00375         jsm_rehash_literals(jsm.lit_capacity * 2);
00376         thread_pop_root();
00377     }
00378 
00379     tm_unlock();
00380     return str;
00381 } // jstring_create_literal()
00382 
00387 java_lang_String_t *jstring_create_from_utf8(const char *src)
00388 {
00389     uintptr_t value;
00390     uint32_t len;
00391 
00392     java_lang_String_t *str;
00393 
00394     if (!utf8_check(src)) {
00395         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00396                 "UTF8 string contains invalid characters");
00397     }
00398 
00399     len = utf8_to_java_length(src);
00400 
00401     if (len == 0) {
00402         value = JNULL;
00403     } else {
00404         value = gc_new_array_nonref(T_CHAR, len);
00405         thread_push_root(&value);
00406     }
00407 
00408     utf8_to_java(array_get_data((array_t *) value), src);
00409     str = JAVA_LANG_STRING_REF2PTR(gc_new(jsm.str_cl));
00410 
00411     if (len != 0) {
00412         thread_pop_root();
00413     }
00414 
00415     str->value = (array_t *) value;
00416     str->count = len;
00417     str->offset = 0;
00418     str->cachedHashCode = 0;
00419     str->next = NULL;
00420 
00421     return str;
00422 } // jstring_create_from_utf8()
00423 
00429 java_lang_String_t *jstring_create_from_unicode(const uint16_t *uchar,
00430                                                 uint32_t length)
00431 {
00432     java_lang_String_t *str;
00433     uintptr_t value;
00434 
00435     if (length == 0) {
00436         value = JNULL;
00437     } else {
00438         value = gc_new_array_nonref(T_CHAR, length);
00439         thread_push_root(&value);
00440     }
00441 
00442     memcpy(array_get_data((array_t *) value), uchar, length * sizeof(uint16_t));
00443     str = JAVA_LANG_STRING_REF2PTR(gc_new(jsm.str_cl));
00444 
00445     if (length != 0) {
00446         thread_pop_root();
00447     }
00448 
00449     str->value = (array_t *) value;
00450     str->count = length;
00451     str->offset = 0;
00452     str->cachedHashCode = 0;
00453     str->next = NULL;
00454 
00455     return str;
00456 } // jstring_create_from_unicode()
00457 
00464 static uint32_t jstring_hash(const uint16_t *data, uint32_t offset,
00465                              uint32_t count)
00466 {
00467     uint32_t hash = 0;
00468     uint32_t limit = count + offset;
00469 
00470     for (size_t i = offset; i < limit; i++) {
00471         hash = (hash * 31) + data[i];
00472     }
00473 
00474     return hash;
00475 } // jstring_hash()
00476 
00481 static bool jstring_equals(const java_lang_String_t *str1,
00482                            const java_lang_String_t *str2)
00483 {
00484     const uint16_t *data1, *data2;
00485 
00486     if (str1 == str2) {
00487         return true;
00488     }
00489 
00490     if (str1->count == str2->count) {
00491         data1 = array_get_data(str1->value);
00492         data2 = array_get_data(str2->value);
00493 
00494         if (memcmp(data1 + str1->offset, data2 + str2->offset,
00495                     str1->count * sizeof(uint16_t)) == 0)
00496         {
00497             return true;
00498         }
00499     }
00500 
00501     return false;
00502 } // jstring_equals()
00503 
00504 #if JEL_PRINT
00505 
00509 void jstring_print(java_lang_String_t *str)
00510 {
00511     uint16_t *data = array_get_data(str->value);
00512     char *tmp = java_to_utf8(data + str->offset, str->count);
00513 
00514     printf(tmp);
00515     gc_free(tmp);
00516 } // jstring_print()
00517 
00518 #endif // JEL_PRINT
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:38:45 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
